category:
Category: Tax

Explanation: This appears to be a tax reconciliation bill, with extensive sections related to tax code modifications, including changes to rates, deductions, credits and exemptions. The document's Section VII specifically focuses on tax-related matters and Internal Revenue Code amendments.

extracted_info.title: An Act to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of H. Con. Res. 14
extracted_info.scope: Tax code modifications, including business tax reforms, international tax provisions, and charitable contribution rules
key_requirements: Establishment of border infrastructure and wall system
New reporting requirements for qualified opportunity funds
Changes to foreign-derived deduction eligible income rules
Modification of business interest deduction limitations

metrics.description: Maximum allowable deduction for qualified tips

key_requirement_details.compliance_action: File tax returns claiming qualified tip deductions

metrics.description: Maximum deduction for qualified overtime compensation (single return)

key_requirement_details.compliance_action: File tax returns claiming overtime compensation deductions

reporting_requirements.description: Annual returns for qualified opportunity funds
Information reporting for qualified opportunity zone businesses

monetary_thresholds.description: Minimum assets threshold for applicable educational institutions excise tax
Income threshold for joint returns regarding tip deduction phase-out

quantitative_limits.description: Minimum charitable contribution floor for individuals

phased_milestones: Most provisions effective for tax years beginning after 2024
Some provisions phase in over 2025-2031 period

notes: This is a comprehensive tax reform act with multiple effective dates and phase-in periods
Many provisions require regulatory guidance from Treasury/IRS for implementation details
